access.user: Can upload in the comment attachment flow; download permissions must be re-validated.
access.admin: No standalone public 'upload management console' currently exists.
access.agent: Can initiate and complete upload metadata workflows and manage owned upload metadata through registered GraphQL operations; binary object transfer remains REST/HTTP-only.

rules.comment-attachments-only: Uploads serve only comment attachments; they are not an independent file space.
rules.permission-and-quota: Permission and quota checks are required before upload.
rules.pure-upload-reads: GET upload-list requests never delete pending reservations. Expired reservations are excluded from quota calculations, while upload create and completion write paths remove a bounded oldest-first batch of expired UploadPending rows only. Request-path reservation cleanup never deletes R2 objects because it cannot coordinate safely with a PUT already in flight; orphan object reconciliation belongs to a dedicated storage lifecycle workflow.
rules.paginated-upload-list: Completed uploads use the shared { data, pagination, meta } list envelope. REST accepts page/pageSize with deprecated limit compatibility, MCP accepts page/limit, and quota plus file-size metadata lives under meta.
rules.write-auth-unsuspended: Upload write routes require an unsuspended signed-in user; suspended users receive 403 before upload metadata or object state changes.
rules.three-step-upload: Web comment attachment uploads follow a three-step flow — create upload session on-site → browser PUT to an on-site Cloudflare Workers upload object route backed by R2 → confirm completion on-site.
rules.r2-backend: Upload object create/read/head/delete operations use the Cloudflare R2 binding. The app runtime does not use S3-compatible signed URL fallbacks.
rules.storage-observability: Shared storage helpers write production-safe Cloudflare Analytics Engine datapoints for storage operation status, duration, and returned object size where available; datapoints identify operation type but do not include bucket names, object keys, signed URLs, filenames, or user IDs.
rules.safe-filenames: Upload create, completion, and rename requests reject ASCII control characters in filenames; download responses use a safe Content-Disposition filename fallback with an encoded UTF-8 filename parameter.
rules.e2e-r2: E2E upload coverage should exercise the same Workers upload object route used in production.
rules.one-upload-one-comment: A completed upload can be attached to at most one comment. Reusing an upload id across comments is rejected instead of treated as implicit file sharing.
rules.download-auth-required: Downloads must not bypass authorization by obtaining a direct link.
rules.download-delivery: /api/workspace/uploads/[id]/download validates owner access or visible comment-attachment access before streaming the object from the R2 binding.
rules.mcp-transfer-boundary: MCP registered GraphQL operations may reserve quota, return the on-site authenticated PUT workflow URL, complete an already-uploaded object, and manage owned metadata. MCP never receives, proxies, or returns raw object bytes; PUT and download delivery remain R2-backed HTTP surfaces. MCP-resource bearer tokens cannot be replayed at the REST PUT URL, so byte-transfer clients need a browser session or separately authorized REST upload token.

routes.returns: { data: Upload[], pagination, meta: { maxFileSizeBytes, quotaBytes, usedBytes } }
notes: The same path currently handles both 'read upload quota / history' and 'create upload session' capabilities.
Accepts page and pageSize; deprecated limit remains an alias for pageSize.

tools.name: workspace_upload_list
tools.returns: { data: Upload[], pagination, meta: { maxFileSizeBytes, quotaBytes, usedBytes } }
notes: Lists completed uploads and quota state for the current user; does not create upload sessions or stream object bytes.
Accepts page and limit.

mutations.name: uploadSessionCreate
notes: Registered operation id: workspace.upload.session.create.v1.
Reserves quota and returns only workflow metadata; no byte input exists.
The operation is marked destructive because it creates an UploadPending quota reservation; its bounded stale-session sweep removes expired reservation rows only.

mutations.name: uploadSessionComplete
notes: Registered operation id: workspace.upload.complete.v1.
Validates the existing R2 object and commits metadata without accepting bytes.

Expiry, quota, missing-object, and oversized-object failures do not delete R2 from the request path. The reservation remains owned until bounded expired-row cleanup, while orphan object reconciliation requires a dedicated storage lifecycle workflow.

routes.returns: { key: String, url: String, maxFileSizeBytes: Int, quotaBytes: Int, usedBytes: Int }
notes: The returned upload URL points to the on-site Workers upload object route and writes through the R2 binding.
Requires an unsuspended signed-in user; suspended users receive 403 before upload session creation.
Writes the pending object to R2 after re-validating auth, key ownership, reservation expiry, and size limits.

routes.returns: { upload: Upload, usedBytes: Int, quotaBytes: Int }
notes: Expiry, quota, missing-object, and oversized-object failures return their existing 400 response without deleting R2 from the request path.
Expired reservation rows are reclaimed in bounded DB-only batches; orphan object reconciliation requires a dedicated storage lifecycle workflow.

tools.name: graphql_operation_run
notes: workspace.upload.session.create.v1 reserves quota and returns the authenticated on-site PUT workflow URL; workspace.upload.complete.v1 completes metadata for an object already uploaded through that HTTP URL.
No MCP input accepts raw object bytes, and MCP-resource bearer tokens cannot be replayed at the REST PUT URL.
Completion failures do not attempt request-path R2 cleanup; expired reservation rows and orphan objects have separate DB and storage lifecycle responsibilities.
Validates owner access or access through a visible attached comment before serving the object from R2.

mutations.name: uploadRename
notes: Registered operation id: workspace.upload.rename.v1.

mutations.name: uploadDelete
notes: Registered operation id: workspace.upload.delete.v1.
Returns SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE when R2 deletion cannot be confirmed and preserves metadata for retry.
Renames one upload owned by the current user.
Requires an unsuspended signed-in user; suspended users receive 403 before upload metadata changes.

Deletes one upload owned by the current user. The backing storage object is deleted before metadata is removed; storage cleanup failure returns an error and leaves the upload metadata available for retry.
Requires an unsuspended signed-in user; suspended users receive 403 before upload metadata or object state changes.

tools.name: workspace_upload_rename
notes: Requires an unsuspended signed-in user and only renames uploads owned by the current user.

tools.name: workspace_upload_delete
tools.returns: { success: Boolean, deletedId: String, deletedSize: Int }
notes: Requires an unsuspended signed-in user, deletes only uploads owned by the current user, removes the backing storage object before metadata deletion, returns storage_delete_failed without deleting metadata when storage cleanup fails, and commits metadata deletion plus the upload_delete audit log together after successful storage deletion.
